2605.18144.title: Evidence-Grounded Frontier Mapping and Agentic Hypothesis Generation in Nanomedicine
2605.18144.paper_summary: Nanomedicine research spans delivery chemistry, immunology, imaging, biomaterials, and disease-specific translational science, yet its conceptual design space remains fragmented across a large and heterogeneous literature. To date, artificial intelligence in nanomedicine has focused primarily on property prediction and formulation optimization, with much less attention to evidence-grounded discovery support at the level of research direction selection. We introduce pArticleMap, a literature-mapping and research-hypothesis-generation system that combines article embeddings, similarity-graph analysis, sparse frontier extraction, structured evidence-pack retrieval, and an audited large-language-model (LLM) workflow for grounded ideation. Rather than forecasting future concept co-occurrence, pArticleMap targets low-density article-level bridge regions and cluster interfaces, then generates and scores citation-grounded hypotheses with large language models in an agentic setup. We evaluate the system with a retrospective realization benchmark (generate later literature under a historical cutoff) and a blinded human reader assessment layer across cue-conditioned nanomedicine tasks. Across 4 selected retrospective bundles, pArticleMap generated ideas and selected task-retained hypotheses (winner ideas) under the benchmark protocol. For task-level retained hypotheses, a pooled gold recovery rate of 10.8% was obtained, with a recall@10 of 15.9% and a future-neighborhood rate of 61.0%, indicating that the system often reached the correct forward-looking neighborhood (paper ideas) even without exact paper-level recovery. Human-agent agreement is modest overall, indicating that internal scoring is useful as a support signal but does not replace expert judgment. These results position pArticleMap as a conservative, evidence-grounded research assistant for nanomedicine.
2605.18144.paper_summary_zh: 奈米醫學研究涵蓋了傳遞化學、免疫學、影像學、生物材料以及特定疾病的轉譯科學，但其概念設計空間在大量異質文獻中仍然分散。迄今為止，奈米醫學中的人工智慧主要集中在性質預測和配方優化上，而對於基於證據的發現支持在研究方向選擇層面上則關注較少。我們介紹了 pArticleMap，這是一個文獻映射和研究假設生成系統，結合了文章嵌入、相似性圖分析、稀疏邊界提取、結構化證據包檢索以及經過審核的大型語言模型（LLM）工作流程以支持有根據的創意。pArticleMap 的目標不是預測未來概念的共現，而是針對低密度的文章級橋接區域和集群界面，然後在代理設置中生成並評分基於引用的假設。 我們通過回顧性實現基準（在歷史截止日期下生成後續文獻）和一個盲人讀者評估層來評估該系統，涵蓋了以提示為條件的奈米醫學任務。在四個選定的回顧性捆綁中，pArticleMap 在基準協議下生成了想法並選擇了任務保留的假設（獲勝想法）。對於任務級保留的假設，獲得了 10.8% 的總金回收率，召回率@10 為 15.9%，未來鄰域率為 61.0%，這表明該系統即使在沒有精確的文章級回收的情況下，仍然經常達到正確的前瞻性鄰域（論文想法）。人類與代理的協議整體上是適度的，這表明內部評分作為支持信號是有用的，但並不能取代專家的判斷。這些結果使 pArticleMap 成為奈米醫學中一個保守的、基於證據的研究助手。
2605.18144.author: Christiaan G. A. Viviers et.al.
2605.18144.authors: Christiaan G. A. Viviers, Koen de Bruin, Mirre M. Trines, Ayla M. Hokke, Roy van der Meel, Avi Schroeder, Twan Lammers, Willem J. M. Mulder, Fons van der Sommen
